creator: rfg@monkeys.com
text:
In message <199911172139.WAA24107@qix.jmz.org>, you wrote:

>Obviously you need the lcircle10 font. Having TeX installed does not
>assure you that you can process any .tex file (in particular LaTeX files
>also require LaTeX).
>In your case install texinfo (/usr/ports/print/texinfo).

Thank you.  Yes!  Sorry to be so silly.

(There's a texinfo.tex file in the same directory where I was running tex
on the termcap.texi file, and I had assumed that that would be sufficient,
but I guess not.)

OK, next question:  Now that I have a nice .dvi file, what FreeBSD package
do i need to intall in order to get the `dvips' utility so that I can turn
this .dvi file into something that's actually usable?

P.S.  Please forgive my abundant ignorance.  I'm relatively new to FreeBSD.
